mechanism_narrative: A rising level of nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) signals ongoing labor market expansion that supports aggregate income growth and corporate earnings, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns following months with positive month-over-month PAYEMS growth are not significantly greater than zero (t-test p > 0.05) over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate is pinned below 0.25 percent, the absence of yield on safe assets drives capital reallocation into equities, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: In periods where FEDFUNDS < 0.25, the proportion of positive 20-day SPX returns is less than 55% over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Joint strength in retail sales (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) indicates synchronized consumer and residential investment momentum that is not captured by either series in isolation, predicting positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal from RSAFS and HOUST does not outperform a simple equal-weighted combination of their independent univariate signals by at least 3 percentage points in annualized Sharpe ratio over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate is elevated above 6 percent, a month-over-month decline in UNRATE signals accelerating labor market repair that reduces recession risk and produces positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: In regimes where UNRATE > 6%, months with declining UNRATE do not produce average 20-day SPX returns that exceed the average return in months with rising UNRATE (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) crossing above the 99 index level signals manufacturing activity has recovered to near pre-crisis capacity, boosting cyclical earnings expectations and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: When INDPRO > 99, the win rate for positive 5-day SPX returns falls below 52% over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Low FEDFUNDS combined with rising RSAFS indicates that monetary stimulus is translating into actual consumer spending; the interaction predicts positive 60-day returns because either condition alone lacks transmission validation.
failure_condition: A regression of 60-day SPX returns on FEDFUNDS, RSAFS, and their two-way interaction term yields an R-squared below 0.02 or a t-stat on the interaction term below 1.5 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) falling below 900 thousand units signals a weakening residential construction pipeline that reduces cyclical growth expectations and predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: When HOUST < 900, the proportion of negative 5-day SPX returns is not significantly greater than 50% (binomial test p > 0.05) over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Higher real GDP (GDPC1) reflects expanded aggregate output that underpins broad-based corporate revenue growth, predicting positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Quarter-over-quarter increases in GDPC1 do not coincide with higher average 20-day SPX returns than quarters with decreasing GDPC1 (t-test p > 0.05) over a 5-year rolling window.
